steps.title: Separate two complete clauses
steps.content: The first clause says Gujarati grammar includes noun cases common to Indo-Aryan languages. The second clause explains the locative case. Since both parts can stand as complete sentences, the boundary needs strong punctuation.

steps.title: Use the semicolon boundary
steps.content: A and B use only a comma, creating a comma splice. D adds 'such as' before a complete clause, which does not fit the sentence structure. A semicolon correctly joins the two independent clauses. The answer is C.
